column2: Laws that address public offenses against the state or society; results in jail/fines.
Laws dealing with disputes between individuals or organizations; results in financial compensation.
Law enacted by government agencies (e.g., FCC, SEC) to enforce compliance.
Legal protection for inventions (utility/design); utility patents typically last 20 years.
Protects expression of ideas (e.g., books, software); lasts life of author + 70 years.
Protects words, slogans, and logos used to identify goods or services.
Proprietary information that provides a competitive edge and is kept confidential.
U.S. law protecting the privacy and security of health information (PHI).
U.S. law requiring strict financial reporting and internal controls for public companies.
U.S. law requiring financial institutions to protect customer PII and share privacy practices.
